note: V9 renders exist but are ~40% smaller in file size (472KB vs 700-865KB). Suggests different render settings or compression. Unable to apply Blender MCP fixes without active Blender server. Audit based on file analysis.

file_size_comparison.interpretation: V9 files are 38% smaller. Possible causes: lower sample count, lower resolution output, aggressive PNG compression, or different render engine settings.

v9_render_analysis.recommendation_for_cycle2: Cannot proceed with Blender MCP fixes without: 1) Active Blender instance on localhost:9876, 2) Ability to load v9_scene1.blend through v9_scene4.blend, 3) Apply fixes and re-render. Current environment does not have Blender MCP server running.

worst_scenes: Scene 2 (Pedestrian): 4.17/10 - Featureless blob pedestrian model is inexcusable
Scene 1 (T-Bone): 5.17/10 - Toy-like vehicles, zero impact deformation
Scene 4 (Hit-Run): 4.67/10 - No damage shown, amateurish text overlay
audit_summary_v4.best_scene: Scene 3 (Highway): 5.83/10 - Lighting saves it from being completely amateur
audit_summary_v4.market_value: $1,500-$2,000 maximum. A lawyer would laugh at Scene 2.

what_would_improve_to_7_plus: GEOMETRY: Replace all vehicles with professional high-poly models (Kenney assets minimum)
PEDESTRIAN: Remove blob, use rigged human character with clothing and proportional anatomy
MATERIALS: Implement full PBR workflow (BaseColor, Normal, Roughness, Metallic maps)
ASPHALT: Use substance painter quality material with weathering, stains, proper specularity
LIGHTING: Three-point Hollywood lighting setup with volumetric shadows
DAMAGE: Add visible crumpling, deformation, broken glass on impact vehicles
DEBRIS: Scatter realistic debris at collision points
ENVIRONMENT: Realistic sky, building occlusion, proper atmospheric perspective
SHADOWS: Ambient occlusion, contact shadows, proper shadow depth
PROFESSIONALISM: Remove all text overlays except case labels, use proper exhibits naming
blocking_issues_for_fixes: No active Blender instance connected to Blender MCP server
localhost:9876 not responding - cannot apply Python fixes through MCP
V9 .blend files exist but cannot be modified without Blender
Recommend: Start Blender with MCP server enabled, then execute apply_audit_fixes.py via MCP call

honest_assessment.expert_witness_credibility: DESTROYED - Scene 2 pedestrian model would be tear-apart point
honest_assessment.litigation_risk: HIGH - opposing counsel would use renders as evidence of poor expert work
honest_assessment.recommendation: DO NOT PRESENT IN COURT without major rebuild. Consider hiring professional forensic animation studio instead.

next_steps: 1. Start Blender 4.0+ with MCP server: blender --listen localhost:9876
2. Load each v9 scene file (v9_scene1.blend through v9_scene4.blend)
3. Execute /tmp/apply_audit_fixes.py via MCP to apply material/lighting fixes
4. Replace vehicle geometry with high-quality models (requires manual asset import)
5. Replace pedestrian blob with rigged human character
6. Re-render at 1920x1080, Cycles 256 samples, use EXR for lossless quality
8. Target: Get to 7.5+ average score before court presentation
